title: Corner Pop
summary:
Foundation corner fracture(s) existed, which are generally the result of differential movement between the
masonry walls (expanding) and the concrete foundation (shrinking).
fullBody:
Foundation corner fracture(s) existed, which are generally the result of differential movement between the
masonry walls (expanding) and the concrete foundation (shrinking). Although this condition did not appear to
adversely affect the structure, sealing these cracks may be desired as they could provide hidden access for
wood destroying insects. Please note that the corners should be examined periodically. If the fracturing
worsens and the corner(s) break off then the brick veneer may lack proper support and repair would be
needed.

title: Settlement / Shrinkage Cracks - Garage Floor
summary: There are settlement / shrinkage cracks visible on the garage floor.
fullBody:
There are settlement / shrinkage cracks visible on the garage floor. This is a common occurrence. Monitoring
these cracks for changes in appearance is recommended.

title: Mechanical surface damage
summary: Damaged shingles were observed on the roof.
fullBody:
Damaged shingles were observed on the roof. This appears to be inadvertent or accidental mechanical
damage that occurred during installation or maintenance. Damaged shingles should be monitored and repaired
or replaced if necessary.

title: Seal caulk joints at wall trim
summary: There are separated caulk joints at the exterior wall trim.
fullBody:
There are separated caulk joints at the exterior wall trim. The joints should be sealed to help prevent moisture
penetration in those areas.

title: Uneven siding
summary: The siding is installed uneven.
fullBody: The siding is installed uneven. Fix or repair as necessary for improved appearance.

title: Mechanical Damage to exterior Walls
summary: There is mechanical damage to the exterior walls or trim.
fullBody: There is mechanical damage to the exterior walls or trim. Repair as necessary.

title: Interior Wall Common Cracks
summary: Common cracks were observed on the interior walls.
fullBody:
Common cracks were observed on the interior walls. This may be due to normal settling and/or thermal
movement of the building materials. These areas should be monitored for further signs of movement. These
areas can be patched and painted as desired for a better appearance.

title: Seal tub / shower surround
summary: Bathtubs /showers are not sealed to the floor or surrounding trim / tile.
fullBody:
Bathtubs /showers are not sealed to the floor or surrounding trim / tile. Sealant should be applied to prevent
moisture from getting underneath the floor and adjacent covering materials. Repair as needed.

title: Damage to Cabinets
summary: Damage was observed to the cabinets or the cabinets are incomplete or unfinished.
fullBody: Damage was observed to the cabinets or the cabinets are incomplete or unfinished. Repair as necessary.

title: Separations at interior trim
summary: There are separations at the interior trim around the house.
fullBody:
There are separations at the interior trim around the house. This normally occurs with seasonal temperature
and humidity changes that cause the wood to expand and contract. Repair as necessary.

title: Wall flashing not properly sealed or installed
summary: The wall flashing is not properly installed or sealed.
fullBody:
The wall flashing is not properly installed or sealed. Moisture was observed under the flashing. The flashing
should be properly repaired and sealed to prevent moisture intrusion or damage.

title: Ceiling Nail Pops
summary: There are nails backing out of the ceilings.
fullBody:
There are nails backing out of the ceilings. This is a common occurrence usually related to expansion and
contraction of the roof rafters and/or seasonal structural movement.

title: Separated caulk joint at interior door trim
summary: There are separated caulk joints at the interior door trim.
fullBody: There are separated caulk joints at the interior door trim. Repair as needed.

title: Seal Garage Door caulk joint
summary: There are separated caulk joints at the overhead garage door casing.
fullBody:
There are separated caulk joints at the overhead garage door casing. The joints should be sealed to help
prevent moisture penetration in those areas.

title: Mechanical Damage to Door Casing / Trim
summary: There is mechanical damage to the door casings and/or trim.
fullBody: There is mechanical damage to the door casings and/or trim. Repair as necessary.

title: Cracked door frame or trim
summary: The door frame or trim is cracked.
fullBody: The door frame or trim is cracked. Fix or repair as necessary.

title: Exterior window deteriorated caulk joint
summary: There are separated caulk joints around the exterior window frames.
fullBody:
There are separated caulk joints around the exterior window frames. This may indicate settling and/or
seasonal movement in those areas. The caulk should be touched up or replaced to exclude pests and moisture
from those areas. Where deteriorating caulk is noted it should be assumed that some moisture penetration has
occurred and that some hidden damage may be present.

title: Window loose or moves around in frame
summary: The kitchen window is loose or moves around in the frame when openings and closing.
fullBody:
The kitchen window is loose or moves around in the frame when openings and closing. The window(s)
should be evaluated by a window specialist and remedied as necessary.

title: Mechanical damage to guard rail
summary: The is mechanical damage to the upstairs guardrail or handrail.
fullBody: The is mechanical damage to the upstairs guardrail or handrail. Repair is recommended.

title: Porch Post mechanical Damage
summary: There is minor damage on the porch posts.
fullBody: There is minor damage on the porch posts. Fix or repair as necessary.

title: Cracks in porch slab
summary: Common cracks, also know as shrinkage cracks, were observed in the exposed areas of the porch.
fullBody:
Common cracks, also know as shrinkage cracks, were observed in the exposed areas of the porch. This
commonly occurs as the result of settling and/or surface checking. Surface checking occurs when concrete is
poured in a dry state, which increases tensile strength. The dry state results in differential curing causing the
surface areas to fracture. This is normal with concrete slabs. Cracks should be monitored for disjointing
and/or separations and evaluated if adverse conditions are observed.

title: Porch ceiling openings
summary: There are openings or separations in the porch ceiling.
fullBody: There are openings or separations in the porch ceiling. Fix or repair as necessary.

title: Fire ants
summary: Fire ants were observed entering or near the structure.
fullBody:
Fire ants were observed entering or near the structure. This can allow moisture to buildup in the wall cavity
and can present a health hazard to those sensitive to fire ant bites. Further evaluation and/or treatment by a
Pest Management Professional is advised.

title: Gate latch missing
summary: The fence gate latch is missing.
fullBody: The fence gate latch is missing. Repair is advised.

title: Lights did not activate
summary: Lights did not activate when tested.
fullBody:
Lights did not activate when tested. This is often due to a burned out bulb or faulty ballast, but a faulty light
fixture cannot be ruled out. It may also be related to an exterior light sensors preventing the light from
coming on or missing ceiling fan remote if applicable. If replacing bulbs does not fix the problem, further
evaluation and/or repair by a licensed electrician is recommended.

title: Missing bulbs
summary: Fixtures are missing the bulbs.
fullBody: Fixtures are missing the bulbs. The fixture was not properly tested as a result. Install bulbs to test fixtures.

title: Dirty air filter
section: III. HEATING, VENTILATION AND AIR CONDITIONING SYSTEMS
summary: The air filter is dirty.
fullBody: The air filter is dirty. Replacement is recommended.

title: Faucet / Escutcheons not sealed
summary: The bathtub and/or shower faucet are not properly sealed to the wall.
fullBody:
The bathtub and/or shower faucet are not properly sealed to the wall. The escutcheons and faucet should be
sealed to the wall to prevent moisture penetration in those areas.

title: Refrigerator or freezer Drawer/Tray missing
summary: There are missing refrigerator or freezer drawers/trays.
fullBody: There are missing refrigerator or freezer drawers/trays. Replacement is advised.

title: Heads spraying on fence
summary: There are sprinkler heads spraying onto the fence.
fullBody:
There are sprinkler heads spraying onto the fence. This can result in damage to the fence over time. The heads
should be properly adjusted to direct spray away from the fence.

title: Heads spraying onto the patio
summary: There are sprinkler heads spraying onto the patio.
fullBody:
There are sprinkler heads spraying onto the patio. The heads should be adjusted to prevent spraying onto the
patio or house.
